2025-09-26.abstract: Mark Carney, Keir Starmer, Pete Buttigieg and Jacinda Ardern were among those at a London summit as a rising populist right appears to threaten progressive politics.

2025-09-26.abstract: A new visa for science and engineering graduates is part of China’s effort to establish itself as the world leader in science and technology.

2025-09-26.title: Abbas’s Message to the United Nations
2025-09-26.abstract: The president of the Palestinian Authority addressed the U.N. General Assembly by video after the U.S. denied him a visa.

2025-09-26.title: Israel Is Flattening Parts of Gaza City
2025-09-26.abstract: The city had been spared from widespread demolitions during previous military operations there. This time is different.

2025-09-27.title: China’s Small Steps Look Bigger Next to Trump’s Retreat
2025-09-27.abstract: Beijing’s climate and trade pledges at the U.N. highlighted how modest moves can stand out when the United States is pulling back from global leadership.

2025-09-27.title: Russia Steps Up Provocations in Europe, Alarming Leaders There
2025-09-27.abstract: European officials fear that Moscow is escalating its antagonism of Europe as U.S. support recedes. A focus of worry now: election interference in Moldova.

2025-09-27.title: Fire at South Korean Data Center Causes Government Service Outages
2025-09-27.abstract: Hundreds of state services were still offline the day after a lithium battery exploded during maintenance work at a data center, officials said. The fire was extinguished on Saturday.

2025-09-27.title: For Want of a Ferryman, a Two-Century U.S.-Canada Link Is Severed
2025-09-27.abstract: With the closing of a crossing between a sleepy Ontario island and a village in New York State, islanders face modest inconvenience — and the end of an era.

2025-09-27.title: Netanyahu’s Defiant U.N. Speech Aimed at Home as Well as the World
2025-09-27.abstract: The Israeli prime minister faced walkouts and protests at the United Nations, but signaled that resisting Palestinian statehood could become the core of his appeal to Israeli voters.

2025-09-28.title: For Many Moldovans, Their Election Is a Choice Between Russia and the E.U.
2025-09-28.abstract: Parliamentary elections in this nation bordering Ukraine come at a critical moment in Moldova’s push for European Union membership.

2025-09-28.title: How China’s Secretive Spy Agency Became a Cyber Powerhouse
2025-09-28.abstract: Fears of U.S. surveillance drove Xi Jinping, China’s leader, to elevate the agency and put it at the center of his cyber ambitions.

2025-09-28.title: Fear and Hope in Venezuela as U.S. Warships Lurk
2025-09-28.abstract: On a visit to Venezuela, The Times found a nation bracing for potential U.S. military action.

2025-09-28.title: Russia Hammers Kyiv in 12-Hour Drone and Missile Assault
2025-09-28.abstract: At least four people were killed in the attack, which also wounded dozens of civilians in the capital and around the country.

2025-09-28.title: What Questions Do You Have About Trump and the World?
2025-09-28.abstract: “Ask a Correspondent” will take your questions to Peter Baker, our chief White House correspondent.

2025-09-29.title: Missing From a U.N. Meeting on Helping Refugees? The Refugees.
2025-09-29.abstract: World leaders will discuss how to assist the Rohingya in the world’s largest refugee settlement. But no one living there is attending the conference at the United Nations.

2025-09-29.title: Tech Outage at Canadian Airports Resolved After Causing Delays
2025-09-29.abstract: Immigration kiosks were offline for hours at major airports and some passengers said they were stranded on planes. The outage was resolved early Monday.

2025-09-29.title: Typhoon Bualoi Slams Into Central Vietnam, Adding to Death Toll
2025-09-29.abstract: At least 51 people were killed and dozens more injured in central Vietnam, where two million were without power. Bualoi’s deadly path had started with 20 deaths in the Philippines.

2025-09-29.title: Here’s the latest.
2025-09-29.abstract: The U.S. President and Prime M

2025-09-29.title: The Missiles Threatening Taiwan
2025-09-29.abstract: China’s rapidly growing missile force threatens Taiwan and the U.S. forces that protect the island.

2025-09-30.title: What It Takes to Get Lunch Delivered to the 70th Floor
2025-09-30.abstract: An informal network of last-mile runners close the gap between harried delivery drivers and hungry office workers in a Shenzhen skyscraper.

2025-09-30.title: How Chinese Weapons Transformed a War Between Two Neighbors
2025-09-30.abstract: China urged Cambodia and Thailand to end their border war in July. But weeks earlier, it had sent rockets and artillery shells to Cambodia, Thai intelligence documents show.

2025-09-30.title: U.S. Deports Planeload of Iranians After Deal With Tehran, Officials Say
2025-09-30.abstract: The deportation flight is one of the clearest signals yet of the Trump administration’s determination to expel migrants, even to places with harsh human rights conditions.

2025-09-30.title: Dozens of Students Are Missing After School Collapses in Indonesia
2025-09-30.abstract: At least three children were killed and about 100 injured after an Islamic boarding school collapsed during a prayer service, officials said.

2025-09-30.title: Secretive Program That Keeps Ukraine’s Weapons Firing Is Suddenly in Doubt
2025-09-30.abstract: A Czech Republic-led effort gathers munitions for Kyiv from around the world, but an opposition party expected to win elections there has vowed to drop it.
